pointers.Delete_signature: engine/btree/delete.go: func Delete(store *NodeStore, alloc *NodeAllocator, rootNodeID uint64, path string) (newRootNodeID uint64, found bool, err error)
pointers.rebalancing_policy_doc: engine/btree/delete.go: Delete's doc comment, section 'Simplified rebalancing strategy (documented choice, per subtask 1.2.4)'
pointers.internal_repair: engine/btree/delete.go: shrinkParentAfterMerge (parent shrink, grandparent splice, root collapse)

pointers.tests: engine/btree/delete_test.go: TestDeleteEmptyTree, TestDeleteAbsentKey, TestDeleteSingleLeaf, TestDeleteLeafMerge, TestDeleteInternalMerge, TestDeleteInsertLookupIntegration, TestDeleteEmptiesSingleLeafTree, TestDelete (literal acceptance entry point, -run TestDelete)

known_gaps_for_downstream: Abandoned/merged-away node IDs are never reused or reclaimed by NodeAllocator (no free-list); expected to be revisited alongside persist/reload (1.2.5/1.2.6).

Leaf-level key-borrow replaces a parent's separator key string in place without re-checking internalEncodedSize; a pathological case with highly variable key lengths could in principle overflow the parent node. Not handled (accepted as out of scope; this subtask's test spec uses uniform-length keys).

Underflow threshold is 'completely empty of keys' (tombstone-until-empty policy), not a numeric half-full threshold -- docs/LLD/btree.md does not specify one, and this is a documented, deliberate simplification per the issue's own suggested wording.
self_consistency: .cdr/runs/2026-07-04/027-implementation/self-consistency.json (build/vet/test all PASS; internal sanity only, NOT independent verification per invariant I4)
